CVE-2012-0880.title: Apache Xerces-C++ allows remote attackers to cause a denial of se ...
CVE-2012-0880.summary: Apache Xerces-C++ allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a crafted message sent to an XML service that causes hash table collisions.

CVE-2018-1311.title: The Apache Xerces-C 3.0.0 to 3.2.3 XML parser contains a use-afte ...
CVE-2018-1311.summary: The Apache Xerces-C 3.0.0 to 3.2.3 XML parser contains a use-after-free error triggered during the scanning of external DTDs. This flaw has not been addressed in the maintained version of the library and has no current mitigation other than to disable DTD processing. This can be accomplished via the DOM using a standard parser feature, or via SAX using the XERCES_DISABLE_DTD environment variable.

CVE-2023-37536.title: An integer overflow in xerces-c++ 3.2.3 in BigFix Platform allows ...
CVE-2023-37536.summary: An integer overflow in xerces-c++ 3.2.3 in BigFix Platform allows remote attackers to cause out-of-bound access via HTTP request.

CVE-2024-23807.title: The Apache Xerces C++ XML parser on versions 3.0.0 before 3.2.5 c ...
CVE-2024-23807.summary:
The Apache Xerces C++ XML parser on versions 3.0.0 before 3.2.5 contains a use-after-free error triggered during the scanning of external DTDs.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 3.2.5 which fixes the issue, or mitigate the issue by disabling DTD processing. This can be accomplished via the DOM using a standard parser feature, or via SAX using the XERCES_DISABLE_DTD environment variable.

This issue has been disclosed before as CVE-2018-1311, but unfortunately that advisory incorrectly stated the issue would be fixed in version 3.2.3 or 3.2.4.
